data.title: European indexes extend gains with Fed decision in focus
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.16% to 9,727. Halifax House Price Index in the UK rose 0.7% Y/Y in November.

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.38% to 23,974. Germany’s factory orders rose 1.5% M/M in October.

France (CAC:IND) +0.26% to 8,142. France trade deficit deteriorates in October. Industrial production in France increased 0.2% M/M in October. France’s official reserve assets climbed to a fresh record of €359.39 billion.

In other parts of Europe, Spain's industrial production rose by 1.2% Y/Y in October.

Norway’s manufacturing production decreased 0.9% M/M in October.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) up 0.19% to 580, with investors awaiting next week’s Federal Reserve policy decision in focus. The central bank is expected to deliver another 25 basis point rate cut next week, with 2–3 more reductions anticipated next year. 

Coming up in the session: Euro Area GDP expected shortly.

In the bond market, the yield on the US 10-year Treasury was unchanged at 3.99%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point at 2.69%.

UK's 10-year yield was unchanged at 4.45%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Fastly prices upsized offering of $160M convertible senior notes
data.description:
• Fastly (FSLY) priced its offering of $160M aggregate principal amount of 0% convertible senior notes due 2030; its offering size was increased from the previously announced $125M.
  • The issuance and sale of the notes are scheduled to settle on December 9, 2025.
  • The initial purchasers have an option to purchase an additional $20M notes.
  • The initial conversion price represents a premium of ~32.5% over the last reported sale price of $11.52 per share of Fastly’s Class A Common Stock on December 4, 2025.
  • Net proceeds will be ~$153.8M (or ~$173.2M if the initial purchasers fully exercise their option to purchase additional notes). Of this, $16.1M will fund the cost of entering into the capped call transactions and the remainder to fund repurchase $150M aggregate principal amount of its outstanding 0% convertible senior notes due 2026. The cap price of the capped call transactions will initially be $23.04 per share.

data.title: Getlink: LeShuttle freight traffic down 7% for November on automotive slump
Baidu's AI chip unit Kunlunxin eyes Hong Kong IPO - report
data.description:
Baidu's (BIDU) AI chip division, Kunlunxin, is planning for a Hong Kong IPO after a recent funding round that valued it at around 21 billion yuan (close to $3 billion), Reuters reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

Kunlunxin aims to submit its listing application to the Hong Kong Stock Exchange as early as Q1 2026. The company wanted to complete an IPO by early 2027, the report said, citing investment materials. This follows a financing round in the last six months, where it raised over 2 billion yuan from investors, including a China Mobile fund.

Founded in 2012 as Baidu's (BIDU) internal AI chip unit, Kunlunxin now operates independently with Baidu (BIDU) retaining a controlling stake. The company has expanded sales driven by U.S. export curbs on advanced chips like Nvidia's (NVDA). 

The company projects 2025 revenue exceeding 3.5 billion yuan, achieving break-even, with over half from external sales beyond Baidu (BIDU), the report added. Its P800 chip powers state-owned data centers, with the new M100 (inference-focused) expected to launch in early 2026 and M300 (training/inference) set for early 2027.

data.title: France trade deficit deteriorates in October
data.description:
• France's trade balance deteriorated slightly (-€0.1 billion) to -€5.2 billion in October 2025,

  • Imports remained stable at €57 billion. Exports decreased slightly (-€0.1 billion).

  • Conversely, the manufactured goods balance worsened (-€0.3 billion), due to a decline in the capital goods balance (-€0.2 billion) and, to a lesser extent, in the intermediate goods balance (-€0.1 billion). The consumer goods balance remained unchanged at -€0.3 billion.

data.title: Hudson Pacific sells Element LA for $231M, revises Q4 FFO outlook to $0.15–$0.25
SoFi Technologies drops 6%, prices $1.5 billion stock offering
data.description:
• SoFi Technologies (SOFI) priced its previously announced underwritten public offering of 54.5M shares at a price of $27.50 per share.
  • Total gross proceeds are expected to be ~$1.5 billion.
  • The underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional ~8.2M shares.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 8, 2025.
  • Net proceeds are expected to be used for general corporate purposes, including but not limited to enhancing capital position, increasing optionality and enabling further efficiency of capital management, and funding incremental growth and business opportunities.
  • The stock price dropped ~6% on Friday during pre-market hours of trading.

data.title: Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacifico's November passenger traffic falls 2%, domestic jumps 4.8% and international falls 10.8%
China's Nvidia-like AI chipmaker Moore Threads jumps ~500% after IPO debut
data.description:
Beijing-based AI chipmaker Moore Threads Technology raised ~$1.13 billion (8 billion yuan) in its Shanghai STAR Market IPO, with shares debuting at 114.28 yuan and soaring as much as 500% on the first day of trading. 

Moore Threads, also called 'China's Nvidia,' benefited from bets that the ​startup will benefit from Beijing's drive to boost domestic chip manufacturing.

Founded in 2020 by ex-Nvidia executive Zhang Jianzhong, Moore Threads shifted from gaming graphics to AI accelerators, capitalizing on U.S. curbs on Nvidia (NVDA) and China's tech self-sufficiency push.

The price-to-sales ratio was at 123 times, higher than the average of 111 times for peers. The chipmaker ⁠expects 2025 sales to jump as much as 242% to 1.5 billion yuan.

The IPO ranks as this year's second-largest onshore offering in China, lagging Huadian New Energy's $2.7 billion figure.

data.title: ServiceTitan outlines $244M–$246M Q4 revenue target as AI-led MAX program scales
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: ServiceTitan, Inc. (TTAN) Q3 2026

Management View

  • Ara Mahdessian, Co-Founder, Chairman of the Board & CEO, highlighted the company's first year as a public entity, stating, "Our growth formula today remains the same as ever. We deliver real ROI to our customers, helping them reach even greater financial outcomes, and this allows them to grow their businesses, which drives more technicians and GTV on our platform and leads to higher subscription and usage revenue for us." Mahdessian emphasized the strategic importance of democratizing AI for the trades and noted, "During the third quarter, year-over-year, we delivered 26% subscription revenue growth and 25% total revenue growth with record free cash flow. Our overall financial performance was greater than we expected due to steady execution with both new and existing customers and strength in usage revenue." He also underscored the role of large enterprise customers, citing the Wrench Group's integration of ServiceTitan's platform and its shift to ServiceTitan's Marketing Pro and Scheduling Pro solutions.
  • Vahe Kuzoyan, Co-Founder, President & Director, reported strong enterprise wins, the introduction of Field Pro as an AI extension for field technicians, and the launch of virtual agents across the Pro portfolio. Kuzoyan stated, "Our Pro product strategy has evolved from delivering powerful functional business automation to delivering a comprehensive platform for agents to automate work across the trades."
  • Dave Sherry, Chief Financial Officer, stated, "Q3 gross transaction volume, or GTV, was $21.7 billion, representing 22% year-over-year growth. Our customers are performing well, again, demonstrating the durability and diversity of the markets we serve, coupled with the ROI that ServiceTitan delivers." Sherry also noted, "Q3 total revenue of $249.2 million grew 25% year-over-year. Subscription revenue of $182.8 million grew 26% year-over-year, led by strong growth in Pro, commercial and new trades. Usage revenue grew 24% year-over-year to $56.8 million, outpacing our expectations due to higher-than-expected fintech utilization."

Outlook

  • Sherry provided guidance for Q4 2026, stating, "For the fourth quarter, we expect total revenue in the range of $244 million to $246 million. We expect to generate operating income in the range of $16 million to $17 million." For the full year fiscal 2026, "we expect total revenue in the range of $951 million to $953 million. We expect to generate operating income in the range of $83 million to $84 million."
  • Sherry referenced seasonality, highlighting "Q4 of this year will have 1 fewer business day as compared to the year ago period, which is expected to compress Q4 FY '26 GTV and usage revenue growth by approximately 150 basis points."

Financial Results

  • Sherry reported, "Q3 professional services revenue was $9.6 million. Net dollar retention was greater than 110% for the quarter. Q3 platform gross margin was 80.2%, an improvement of 310 basis points year-over-year... Total gross margin for Q3 was 74.3%, up 390 basis points year-over-year. Q3 operating income of $21.5 million resulted in operating margin of 8.6%, an improvement of 780 basis points year-over-year."
  • Free cash flow for Q3 reached $38 million, with year-to-date free cash flow at $50 million. Sherry noted a $20 million cash outlay for the acquisition of Conduit completed in October, which presents a cross-sell opportunity, particularly within the residential HVAC segment.

Q&A

  • Kasthuri Rangan, Goldman Sachs, asked about scaling to a multibillion-dollar revenue company and adjustments to the go-to-market approach. Mahdessian responded, "The biggest opportunity that we see relates to AI in the trades. It's a very exciting and critical opportunity for us. It is why it is our #1 priority... customers want one platform that fully automates all of their key workflows... And this is the essence of the MAX program that we announced at Pantheon."
  • Josh Baer, Morgan Stanley, inquired about the MAX program pilot and private equity customer contribution. Kuzoyan answered, "We're still early days on the MAX program, and we are intentionally slow rolling it to make sure we get it right... our private equity customers are our best utilizers of the product. They adopt Pro products to a greater degree, and they grow on average 500 bps faster than non-sponsored backed customers."
  • David Hynes, Canaccord, questioned the efficiency unlock from automation. Mahdessian explained that as businesses automate, "you not only increase profitability, but you actually increase revenue because a lot of these AI automations help increase close rates, average tickets, lead generation and the like."
  • Dylan Becker, William Blair, asked about the roofing initiative and insurance integrations. Kuzoyan noted partnerships with Verisk for claims management and progress in scaling implementation for roofing customers.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were generally positive, focusing on AI initiatives, product adoption, and new market opportunities. Several questions centered on the MAX program and the commercial expansion, with a tone of curiosity and cautious optimism.
  • Management maintained a confident tone throughout, particularly during prepared remarks. Mahdessian stated, "I have also never been more confident that our opportunity to build the operating system for the trades is only just beginning." In Q&A, management was transparent about being "intentionally slow rolling" the MAX program and acknowledged areas for further product and market development.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained optimistic but highlighted the intensifying focus on AI and automation. Analyst questions shifted slightly toward the impact and timing of new strategic programs.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q3 saw a continuation of strong revenue growth, with total revenue increasing to $249.2 million from $242.1 million in Q2. Subscription revenue growth remained robust, though the pace slightly moderated from 27% in Q2 to 26% in Q3.
  • The company's operating income decreased from $29.2 million in Q2 to $21.5 million in Q3, with margin compression attributed to seasonal patterns and increased investment in growth initiatives.
  • Management raised full-year revenue and operating income guidance compared to Q2, reflecting ongoing momentum.
  • Strategic focus shifted more explicitly to scaling AI-driven automation through the MAX program and expanding commercial and roofing capabilities.
  • Analyst sentiment remained positive but increasingly probed the specifics and scalability of new initiatives.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited the impact of one fewer business day in Q4 as a potential headwind for sequential revenue and GTV growth.
  • Analysts inquired about seasonality, product adoption rates, and execution on new programs such as MAX.
  • Sherry described a "prudent GTV forecast" for Q4 and reaffirmed the company's focus on balancing growth and margin expansion, noting that hiring is catching up to plan and may affect incremental margin upside next year.

Final Takeaway

ServiceTitan's Q3 2026 results reflect strong momentum in subscription and usage revenue, with management spotlighting AI-led automation as the core driver of future growth. The company is advancing its MAX program, expanding its Pro product portfolio, and deepening its presence in commercial and roofing trades, while maintaining disciplined execution and raising full-year outlook. Management underscores that the opportunity for durable, compounding growth in the trades sector is only just beginning, with focused investments in AI and automation positioned to transform customer operations and sustain the company's competitive lead.
